name: Zacatecas (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Zacatecas centred.
history: Zacatecas has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

The arms reference the Cerro de la Bufa overlooking the city of Zacatecas, the silver-mining wealth that drove the colonial settlement of the region from 1546, and the Spanish royal grant from Philip II that originally bestowed the arms on the city.
